creator: lichray@gmail.com
text:
On Mon, May 12, 2014 at 8:36 PM, Koichiro IWAO <meta@vmeta.jp> wrote:
> My patch changes category of this port. fcitx is originally developed
> as a chinese input method but now also used for other languages. Category
> should be textproc, like ibus, uim, scim and others.

Someone brought this to me before, and we discussed it.  Indeed
Fcitx has became a framework to allow multilingual input method
to work on.  However, fcitx the main package still has built-in
Chinese input methods, and some Chinese users still look for
fcitx for this purpose.  My plan was, until the upstream decided
to split out the Chinese IM from fcitx the main package, we don't
move the ports to textproc/, or try to split it by ourselves.  The
situation is not yet changed, so I don't like to change.

BTW, Hiroto and me have been working on upgrading the fcitx
dependency tree,

  https://github.com/HirotoKagotani/freebsd-fcitx

We are temporary blocked by one ports which is not managed
by us, but the work on side is done and tested.

-- 
Zhihao Yuan, ID lichray
The best way to predict the future is to invent it.
